name: Game Design Documentation Suite
description: do all .md game design, player, listener and other .md

goal.answer: Create a complete, formal game design documentation suite from the existing source material (GAME_DESIGN.md, Listener.md, player.md), following the CCGS skill templates and a consistent gothic horror-comedy tone. The deliverable is a set of Markdown documents covering the game concept, systems index, full game design document, player systems, Listener AI, project architecture, and implementation control manifest — establishing a single source of truth for all future implementation ferments.

criteria.answer:
`design/gdd/game-concept.md` exists with all 8 CCGS-required sections: Overview, Player Fantasy, Detailed Rules, Formulas, Edge Cases, Dependencies, Tuning Knobs, Acceptance Criteria.
`design/gdd/systems-index.md` exists listing all MVP systems with priorities and current implementation status.
`design/gdd/game-design-document.md` exists as the comprehensive GDD derived from `GAME_DESIGN.md` (1509 lines), with all 18 sections represented.
`docs/player.md` exists as the formal player systems doc derived from existing `player.md`, covering movement, voice mechanics, Token, roles, death/spectator.
`docs/listener.md` exists as the formal Listener AI doc derived from existing `Listener.md`, covering states, senses, behavior tree, escalation.
`docs/architecture/architecture.md` exists describing the project code layer structure (Core, Player, Enemy, World, UI, Networking).
`docs/architecture/control-manifest.md` exists with technical implementation rules (no hardcoded values, delta-time, signals-only cross-system, testable logic, no static singletons).
Tone consistency across all docs matches `GAME_DESIGN.md` §7.1/§7.3 gothic horror-comedy register.
All docs are plain Markdown with no broken internal links.

constraints.answer: No code changes in this ferment — documentation only., No new dependencies or Godot plugins., All tone must match the gothic horror-comedy register established in GAME_DESIGN.md §7.1 and §7.3., Use CCGS templates from `claude-code-game-studios/.claude/docs/templates/` as the structural baseline., Do not delete existing `GAME_DESIGN.md`, `Listener.md`, or `player.md` — they remain source material.

phases.answer: Core GDDs, System Docs, Architecture Docs, Cross-Cut & Link Check

assumptions.answer: `GAME_DESIGN.md` (1509 lines, 18 sections) is the authoritative source of truth for the full GDD.; `Listener.md` and `player.md` are implementation-audit documents that will be reformatted and elevated into formal system design docs.; CCGS templates (`game-concept.md`, `systems-index.md`, `game-design-document.md`, etc.) provide the target structure; deviations are acceptable if the existing source material doesn't map 1:1.; The user does not require implementation in this ferment — only formal documentation.; Internal links are verified against relative file paths inside the project root.

phases.name: Core GDDs
phases.goal: Create the top-level design documents: game concept, systems index, and full GDD.

steps.description: Create `design/gdd/` directory and `design/gdd/game-concept.md` using the CCGS `game-concept.md` template. Extract core fantasy, pillars, unique mechanics, and target experience from GAME_DESIGN.md §1-2.

verification.command: test -f design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Overview' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Player Fantasy' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Detailed Rules' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Formulas' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Edge Cases' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Dependencies' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Tuning Knobs' design/gdd/game-concept.md && grep -q '## Acceptance Criteria' design/gdd/game-concept.md

steps.description: Create `design/gdd/systems-index.md` using the CCGS template. List every MVP system from GAME_DESIGN.md §3-11 with priority (P0/P1/P2), current status (Done/Partial/Not started), owner script, and blocker.

verification.command: test -f design/gdd/systems-index.md && grep -q 'P0\|P1\|P2' design/gdd/systems-index.md && grep -q 'Status' design/gdd/systems-index.md && grep -q '## Systems' design/gdd/systems-index.md

steps.description: Create `design/gdd/game-design-document.md` as the formal, comprehensive GDD. Reorganize GAME_DESIGN.md into the CCGS `game-design-document.md` template (Overview, Player Fantasy, Core Loop, Systems, Content, UI/HUD, Audio, etc.). Preserve all 18 original sections and tone.

phases.name: System Docs
phases.goal: Create formal player and Listener AI system design documents.

steps.description: Create `docs/player.md` using CCGS template. Reformat existing `player.md` content into 8 required sections, adding Player Fantasy and design-doc references. Cover movement, camera, voice/Token, roles, death/spectator, controls.

verification.command: test -f docs/player.md && grep -q '## Overview' docs/player.md && grep -q '## Detailed Rules' docs/player.md && grep -q '## Acceptance Criteria' docs/player.md

steps.description: Create `docs/listener.md` using CCGS template. Reformat existing `Listener.md` content into 8 required sections, adding Player Fantasy and design-doc references. Cover design intent, state machine, senses, behavior tree, escalation, adaptive evolution (deferred).

verification.command: test -f docs/listener.md && grep -q '## Overview' docs/listener.md && grep -q '## Detailed Rules' docs/listener.md && grep -q '## Acceptance Criteria' docs/listener.md

phases.name: Architecture Docs
phases.goal: Create architecture and control-manifest documents for future implementation.

steps.description: Create `docs/architecture/` directory and `docs/architecture/architecture.md`. Define code layer structure: Core (GameManager, VoiceManager, NetworkManager), Player (PlayerController, CameraManager), Enemy (ListenerAI, BT), World (WordPool, WordRegistry, NoteItem, SequenceManager, RadioBroadcast), UI (HUDManager, MainMenu), Networking. Map each layer to existing Scripts/ directories.

verification.command: test -f docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'Core' docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'Player' docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'Enemy' docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'World' docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'UI' docs/architecture/architecture.md && grep -q 'Networking' docs/architecture/architecture.md

steps.description: Create `docs/architecture/control-manifest.md` with technical implementation rules derived from `claude-code-game-studios/.claude/rules/gameplay-code.md`, `test-standards.md`, and `coding-standards.md`. Cover: data-driven values, delta-time, signals-only cross-system, testable public APIs, no static singletons for game state, file headers, design-doc references, conventional commits.

verification.command: test -f docs/architecture/control-manifest.md && grep -q 'data-driven\|hardcoded' docs/architecture/control-manifest.md && grep -q 'delta' docs/architecture/control-manifest.md && grep -q 'signal' docs/architecture/control-manifest.md && grep -q 'singleton' docs/architecture/control-manifest.md

phases.name: Cross-Cut & Link Check
phases.goal: Ensure tone consistency and valid internal links across all new docs.

steps.description: Run tone pass: sample-read each new doc for gothic horror-comedy register; fix any tonal breaks or inconsistent phrasing.

verification.command: grep -R 'absurd\|gothic\|Ashford\|estate' design/gdd/game-concept.md docs/player.md docs/listener.md | wc -l | awk '$1 >= 3 {exit 0} {exit 1}'

steps.description: Run link validation: scan all new docs for relative Markdown links and verify target files exist. Report any broken links.

verification.command: find design docs -name '*.md' -exec grep -H ']([^)]*)' {} + | grep -v 'http' | sed -n 's/.*](\([^)]*\)).*/\1/p' | while read target; do [ -z "$target" ] || [ -f "$target" ] || { echo "BROKEN: $target"; exit 1; }; done

goal: Create a complete, formal game design documentation suite from the existing source material (GAME_DESIGN.md, Listener.md, player.md), following the CCGS skill templates and a consistent gothic horror-comedy tone. The deliverable is a set of Markdown documents covering the game concept, systems index, full game design document, player systems, Listener AI, project architecture, and implementation control manifest — establishing a single source of truth for all future implementation ferments.

No new dependencies or Godot plugins.

Use CCGS templates from `claude-code-game-studios/.claude/docs/templates/` as the structural baseline.
